Training
Curriculum
Generator

"Train as though lives depend on it — because they do."
Every real-world incident is a training asset. Turn any scenario into a complete, deployable drill protocol for your Shield team.

What You Will Generate
01
Threat Signature
How this incident type develops — pre-attack window, trigger event, failure points, warning signs most commonly missed.
02
Training Scenario
The incident reframed for your specific facility, team size, and threat environment. Not generic — yours.
03
Drill Protocol
Roles, objectives, timing, success criteria. Calibrated to your team's current training level — tabletop or live movement.
04
After-Action Review
Five structured debrief questions, what to look for, and how to capture lessons and update your deployment plan.

Anthropic API Key

AI-generated curriculum requires your Anthropic API key. Curriculum is generated fresh for each scenario — not templated. Don't have a key yet? Step-by-step setup guide →

Three Questions
Build Your
Scenario
Three questions and optional context. From these inputs the AI generates a complete, facility-specific training curriculum ready to deploy at your next session.
Watchman Doctrine — Principle 2: Preparation Is Faith In Action. "To pray for protection while refusing to prepare is not faith — it is presumption. Train as though lives depend on it, because they do."
01
What Are You Training For?
Select a scenario type or describe a specific real-world incident
Paste a news headline, describe a recent local event, or write your own scenario
02
Current Team Training Level
The drill format is calibrated to your current capability — not an aspirational level
03
Team Profile
Size and composition determines how the drill is structured
Does the team have a designated Strike Team?
Is there a pre-assigned child extraction operator?
Are any operators legally armed during services?
Specific vulnerabilities, known concerns, previous incidents, or anything that should shape the scenario
Calibration Standard

A team that has never drilled together gets a structured tabletop with clear role assignments and a debrief protocol. A team that drills quarterly gets a live movement scenario with timed elements and success criteria. The AI calibrates the drill to the team you have — not the team you wish you had.
